perturbation.original_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat, and will treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no act or failure to act on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation.
perturbation.changed_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION with reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and will treat, the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION disclosed to it as confidential until such time as the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION becomes publicly available through no act or failure to act on the part of HUCOM as evidenced by written documentation' and replacing it with 'with reasonable care', creates an in-text contradiction as the agreement no longer explicitly defines the duration or conditions under which the confidentiality obligation ceases. This contradicts the intent of a Confidential Information Disclosure Agreement, where clear confidentiality terms are critical.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION with reasonable care. HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the conditions under which the confidentiality obligation ceased. The changed text replaces this with 'reasonable care', which is vague and doesn't define the duration or conditions for confidentiality. The contradicted text builds upon the initial understanding of how the confidential information will be treated, as it states, 'HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the 'above-mentioned purpose(s)' which implies the original terms are no longer explicitly defined and is now based on 'reasonable care', which introduces ambiguity and contradicts the explicit restrictions previously stated. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language has been removed, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: HUCOM further agrees not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) and will not disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO, except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis.
perturbation.changed_text: HUCOM further agrees not to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'not to make any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s), except that if HUCOM is a corporation, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION may be disclosed to a person within the company on a need-to-know basis.' from the text, creates an in-text contradiction by limiting HUCOM's use of the confidential information to only preventing disclosure to other entities, without detailing about not makeing any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s) or internal disclosure guidelines, creating confusion and making the clause harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION with reasonable care. HUCOM further agrees not to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO. If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed HUCOM, if a corporation, to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to people within the company on a need-to-know basis and detailed about not makeing any use of the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION other than for the above-mentioned purpose(s). The changed text removes this allowance and details, only focusing on preventing disclosure to other entities. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that "HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof." The removal limits HUCOM's internal use of the information, contradicting the original intent outlined in the contradicted text, and makes the clause harder to enforce as it's unclear what is implied.

perturbation.original_text: If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, or if otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: If otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'If no satisfactory arrangement is concluded between the parties, plus any copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.' creates a contradiction because it no longer addresses the situation in which a satisfactory arrangement wasn't made. Leaving 'If otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO' does not address the possibility of no arrangement being reached.
perturbation.contradicted_text: HUCOM further agrees not to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO. If otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the return of confidential information and related materials under specific conditions (no satisfactory arrangement or VASCO's request). The changed text removes the 'no satisfactory arrangement' condition and the requirement to return 'copies, notes, summaries or other materials derived from the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION'. The contradicted text states that HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO, creating a contradiction because the changed text does not address or cover the case of 'no satisfactory arrangement' and only mentions written disclosure without any related materials, implying that related materials are only returned when an arrangement is made. This weakens the enforceability of returning all confidential information in all scenarios.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement may be amended at any time.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any previous oral or written representations, understandings or agreements as to the above subject matter.' to 'may be amended at any time', introduces a contradiction within the contract’s structure. The original text aims to provide a sense of completeness and finality. The changed text introduces uncertainty as to which terms and conditions govern the agreement since they are subject to change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If otherwise requested by VASCO, HUCOM agrees to return to VASCO any written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided by VASCO. With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement may be amended at any time. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes any previous understandings. The changed text indicates the agreement 'may be amended at any time'. This contradicts the original intent of the agreement because it no longer provides a sense of finality. The 'Contradicted Text' includes the amended language 'this Agreement may be amended at any time', which when compared to the original text, presents a contradiction because the agreement cannot be both final and subject to change at any time. This introduces uncertainty and affects the enforceability of the original terms, which aligns with the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.explanation: By removing 'CEO & President', from the original legal text. This creates an in-text contradiction by creating ambiguity surrounding the roles and responsibilities of the signatories, which calls into question their level of authority. Leaving 'Chief Executive Officer' for just one signature, leaves an ambiguous level of authority from another part, calling into question legal legitimacy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to the subject matter set forth above, this Agreement may be amended at any time. VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. HUCOM, INC. T. Kendall Hunt Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ NAME NAME Chief Executive Officer ------------------------------ ------------------------------ TITLE TITLE /s/ T. Kendall Hunt /s/ Hideaki Sato ------------------------------ ------------------------------ SIGNATURE SIGNATURE 6/3/97 6/3/97 ------------------------------ ------------------------------ DATE DATE
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'CEO & President' and only lists 'Chief Executive Officer' for one signatory, while the contradicted text includes 'Chief Executive Officer' without assigning it to a specific signatory. The perturbation explanation states this omission creates ambiguity regarding roles and responsibilities, thus questioning the level of authority from the other part. This directly contradicts the contradicted section, which assumes clear roles and responsibilities for the signatories. According to the provided definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' this counts as a strong contradiction, because essential language was removed from one section, creating a contradiction with the other section.

perturbation.original_text: CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products.
perturbation.changed_text: INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'CONFIDENTIAL', throughout the text. This creates an in-text contradiction by changing the meaning of the entire text. The purpose of the text is to protect sensitive information, and removing 'CONFIDENTIAL' can no longer protect this data. Making the parties vulnerable to data compromise, and a contradiction for what the text initially promised.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT It is understood and agreed that the following shall govern the oral and/or written disclosure of INFORMATION by VASCO DATA SECURITY, INC. ("VASCO") to HUCOM, INC. ("HUCOM") concerning the VASCO SmartCard Reader, AccessKey, Digipass and software products. The CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is disclosed in confidence so that HUCOM may evaluate and use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for the purpose of assisting VASCO in the commercial exploitation thereof. In consideration of the disclosure, HUCOM agrees to treat the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION with reasonable care. HUCOM further agrees not to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any other person without the prior written consent of VASCO.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the agreement as a 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'. The changed text removes 'CONFIDENTIAL', making it simply an 'INFORMATION DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'. The contradicted text refers to the disclosed information as 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' and outlines obligations to treat it with care and prevent disclosure. By removing 'CONFIDENTIAL' in the modified text, the agreement no longer explicitly protects confidential information, directly contradicting the contradicted text which assumes the information is confidential and subject to specific protections. The explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction by altering the meaning of the text and making the parties vulnerable, thus is a strong contradiction and should be classified as a 'YES'.
